Full night's rest after a balanced day of work, spontaneity, and leisure; Sven gets solid sleep before the next day.

diary: Monday started early with that perfect June light streaming through the kitchen. I made my usual bagel and coffee in Redmond before the drive in—I-90 was mercifully clear. Got through some corridor planning work this morning, but honestly, I couldn't stop thinking about the World Cup match. At 10 AM I caved and walked over to Monkey Loft with a couple colleagues. The bar was already buzzing. We grabbed beers and sandwiches, and I got caught up in the energy of it all—USA playing Wales, the place packed with people who actually cared. By the time I walked back to the office, I was buzzing too, though the afternoon meetings brought me back down to earth. The drive home was gorgeous though, all golden light on the freeway. Picked up some fresh fish and vegetables at Whole Foods, cooked a decent dinner, and spent the evening painting in the backyard. The weather held perfectly. Felt good to just be present for once.
thought_process: I started the day in my usual rhythm, but I knew the match was happening and I could feel that pull. Normally I'm disciplined about work, but something about a sunny Monday in early June and the World Cup made me think—why not? It's rare I let myself take a real break mid-day. The light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drove, but it made me think about how fragile our systems are, even with maintenance. By afternoon, the buzz from the bar had worn off and I was back in serious mode for design reviews. The commute home felt like a reset. Cooking and painting settled something in me—I think I needed that balance between the social rush and quiet creation.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
